reason: All 4 acceptance criteria independently verified: files[:7] confirmed in source, food_011 absent from breakfast in live planner run, 0/7 plans contained food_011, and all 5 unit tests pass. Note: git was blocked during build (git_blocked=true, before==after commit); change is on-disk only with no git commit.

criterion_results.criterion: nutrition_app/agents/agent_5_planner/meal_planner.py: _load_recently_used() expands its lookback from 3 to at least 7 plan files
criterion_results.evidence: Direct inspection of line 141 confirms 'for f in files[:7]:'; docstring at line 133 reads 'Load food IDs used in the last 7 plans for rotation'. No files[:3] found in file.

criterion_results.criterion: After the change, running the planner against the existing food catalog produces a new plan where food_011 is absent from the meal that food_011 most recently occupied (breakfast)
criterion_results.evidence: Independently ran MealPlanner with 241-food catalog loaded; recently_used contains 80 foods including food_011 (loaded from 7 most recent plans). Generated plan breakfast=['food_052','food_080','food_085','food_086','food_089'] — food_011 absent.

criterion_results.criterion: food_011 appearance rate across any sliding window of 7 consecutive generated plans does not exceed 2 occurrences (<=2/7)
criterion_results.evidence: Independently generated 7 consecutive plans with live catalog; food_011 appeared in 0/7 (well within <=2 limit). food_011 consistently penalized as recently_used in every planner instantiation since it appears in all 7 loaded plan files.

criterion_results.criterion: All existing unit tests in nutrition_app/tests/unit/test_meal_planner.py continue to pass with no modifications to the test file
criterion_results.evidence: Ran pytest independently: 5 tests collected (test_generates_meals, test_plan_has_items, test_plan_respects_run_id, test_plan_has_calories, test_empty_plan_fails_validation) — all PASSED in 0.21s. No test file modifications.
